---
output: github_document
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# BSET

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

`BSET` (Bayesian Surrogate Evaluation Test) is an `R` package for validating surrogate markers in clinical trials. It implements the imputation-based Bayesian methodology proposed by Carlotti and Parast (2026), extending the frequentist rank-based framework of Parast et al. (2024). The package supports Bayesian testing both with and without baseline covariates. Additionally, it includes comprehensive simulation suites to replicate studies from both papers, enabling performance comparisons between Bayesian and frequentist approaches across diverse clinical scenarios.

## Installation

You can install the development version of BSET from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("PietroCarlotti/BSET")
```
